title: Slow Simmered Spanish Chicken Paella
description: This earthy and indulgent Spanish dish features chicken, rice, and vegetables in a rich and flavorful broth, made with plain flour and low-fat ingredients, and is low-fat-friendly as it uses minimal oil and no heavy sauces.
instructions: Blanch the green beans in boiling water for 3 minutes, then set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika and saffron to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the flour to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, diced tomatoes, and artichoke hearts to the pan, and stir to combine.
Add the rice to the pan, stirring to combine, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the blanched green beans and lemon juice to the pan, and stir to combine.
Continue to simmer, covered, for an additional 10 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
